identificationModule.briefTitle: Flow Regulation by Opening the Septum in Patients With Heart Failure Trial
identificationModule.officialTitle: Flow Regulation by Opening the Septum in Patients With Heart Failure (FROST-HF) Trial

descriptionModule.briefSummary: To investigate the safety and efficacy of a novel atrial flow regulator (AFR) device in patients with heart failure and preserved ejection fraction.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription: The study will allow us to demonstrate the safety of the AFR device as well as determine the clinical response of the therapeutic intervention. In addition, the invasive exercise hemodynamics will provide objective data for the changes in the cardiac hemodynamics and delineate underlying mechanisms of creating a permanent inter atrial shunting.

conditions: Heart Failure With Normal Ejection Fraction

designInfo.interventionModelDescription: Multicenter, randomized, sham controlled, single blind with objective end points.

armGroups.label: Control arm
armGroups.description: Sham procedure to include cardiac catheterization and hemodynamic. Ongoing management at the discretion of the treating physician. Patient to undergo

armGroups.label: AFR arm
armGroups.description: Implantation of Occlutech atrial flow regulator (AFR) device

interventions.name: AFR implantation
interventions.description: Implantation of atrial flow regulator (AFR) device

interventions.name: Sham procedure
interventions.description: Sham procedure to include cardiac catheterization and hemodynamic. Ongoing management at the discretion of the treating physician. Patient to undergo

primaryOutcomes.description: Six minute walk test at 12 months

secondaryOutcomes.description: Composite of cardiac death, heart failure hospitalization and worsening heart failure
Death from a cardiac cause (myocardial infarction, sudden death)
Heart failure hospitalization, worsening heart failure

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Informed consent
* Age \> 40 years
* LVEF \> 45% documented within the last 3 months.
* NYHA class ≥II status or 6MWT distance \<80% predicted.
* Left atrial enlargement
* PCWP ≥25 mmHg during supine bicycle exercise on hemodynamic monitoring.
* Left atrial pressure greater than right atrial pressure by ≥5 mmHg on hemodynamic monitoring.

Exclusion Criteria:

* Recent MI, PCI or CABG
* Untreated CAD with indication for revascularization
* Recent stroke, pulmonary embolism or major surgery
* Uncontrolled atrial fibrillation
* H/O cardiomyopathy (hypertrophic, restrictive, infiltrative) or pericardial disease
* Inability to perform a 6-minute walk test.
* Clinically significant valvular disease
* Uunsuitable for study participation at investigator discretion.
* Severe COPD, Anemia or morbid obesity
* Uncontrolled hypertension
* Women of child bearing age
* RA pressure \>15 mmHg or PVR \>4 wood units
